claim: An operator's context budget shall measure the SIZE of its resident business content in bytes/chars (tokens when the host exposes them), not node counts.
why: M17. Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «наверное, это размер бизнес-требований. Лучше в байтах или в токенах» (English: 'probably it's the size of the business requirements. Better in bytes or in tokens'). Landed reality already matches: BudgetMeasureCRYSTAL_CHARS ('measure = len(root CLAUDE.md) in characters') in internal/ontology/operator.go is the current byte-approximation of resident business content, wired into check_operator_within_budget (internal/invariants/lifecycle_checks.go) and used by the live meta-domain operator. BudgetMeasureTOKEN_ESTIMATE is declared in the BudgetMeasures map (internal/ontology/operator.go) as the token-exact seam the steward names ('в токенах') for when the host exposes token counts, but is deferred (not measured yet) since no host API is wired. BudgetMeasureNODE_COUNT (the original M17 default, size = len(requirements)+len(conflicts)+len(assumptions)) is DEMOTED to a legacy measure: it counts the crystallized SUBSTRATE, which R-working-vs-substrate-budget declares free, so it over-penalizes crystallizing; it remains selectable only for operators/tests that opted into it before CRYSTAL_CHARS existed.
